title: Authorize and prove first Restic maintenance run
goal: After an approved retention policy, execute at most one explicitly authorized maintenance run and prove repository health, retained restore capability and clean post-state without enabling the weekly timer.

approval.note: Registration only. The actual forget/prune/check effect requires a current reviewed policy, exact confirmation hash and separate operator authorization at execution time.

acceptance.assertion: Backup service is inactive, repository lock is free, latest exact restore proof is fresh, retirement remains valid and the reviewed policy hash matches the deployed unit.
One current operator authorization names the exact policy SHA-256 and permits one maintenance execution only.
Exactly one forget/prune/check run executes through the commit-bound wrapper; no timer activation, unrelated snapshot target or concurrent backup occurs.
Repository check passes, lock/tunnel/temp state is clean, daily backup remains enabled and maintenance timer remains inactive and linked.
A protected retained snapshot is restored exactly and its sentinel or equivalent evidence remains byte-identical after maintenance.

does_not_establish: safe deletion beyond the reviewed policy
required_preconditions_before_effect: Install the reviewed source policy through a new commit-bound reversible Infra runtime release.
Verify the deployed maintenance wrapper and systemd unit both report policy SHA-256 c7d1bad1f0d1f35d602541082131568848e55784880457acdeff315e06965254.
Obtain a fresh exact-snapshot restore proof and repository check after deployment.
Obtain a new operator authorization naming the exact policy SHA-256 and permitting one maintenance run only.
Keep the weekly maintenance timer inactive and linked before and after the one-shot run.
